2606.20274.title: Lagrange: An Open-Vocabulary, Energy-Based Sparse Framework for Generalized End-to-End Driving
2606.20274.paper_summary: Scaling end-to-end autonomous driving to complex, open-world environments requires perceptual models that generalize to anomalous scenarios and planners that produce kinematically valid trajectories. Existing paradigms face a distinct dichotomy between representational efficiency and generalization capacity. Dense models (e.g., occupancy networks), while geometrically robust, incur critical computational bottlenecks and struggle with high-level semantic reasoning. Conversely, sparse, query-based planners are efficient but reliant on closed-set definitions, rendering them vulnerable to out-of-distribution (OOD) events. Although recent Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models offer open-vocabulary reasoning, their autoregressive, discrete token generation fundamentally conflicts with the continuous, high-frequency control requirements of vehicle dynamics. To address this, we propose Lagrange, an open-vocabulary, computationally sparse driving framework based on Masked Latent Fields (MLF). Rather than relying on dense volumetric reconstructions or closed-set query mechanisms, Lagrange exploits Vision-Language Models (VLMs) to encode class-agnostic object proposals into continuous semantic visual tokens. We introduce an intent-driven masked cross-attention module that temporally filters irrelevant entities, decoding the attended tokens into an implicit continuous energy field defined over spatial coordinates. By framing decision-making as a Lagrangian action minimization problem spanning this energy field, we enforce strict compliance with vehicle kinematics while executing collision avoidance. Extensive offline evaluations on both standard (nuScenes) and long-tail (CODA) benchmarks demonstrate that Lagrange establishes a promising framework for robust, interpretable, and kinematically feasible open-world autonomy.
2606.20274.paper_summary_zh: 擴展端到端自主駕駛至複雜的開放世界環境需要能夠對異常情境進行概括的感知模型和能產生運動學有效軌跡的規劃器。現有的範式面臨著表徵效率和概括能力之間的明顯二分法。雖然密集模型（例如，佔用網絡）在幾何上穩健，但會產生關鍵的計算瓶頸，並且在高層次語義推理方面表現不佳。相反，稀疏的基於查詢的規劃器雖然高效，但依賴於封閉集定義，使其容易受到分佈外（OOD）事件的影響。儘管最近的視覺-語言-行動（VLA）模型提供了開放詞彙的推理，但其自回歸的離散標記生成與車輛動力學的連續高頻控制需求根本上存在衝突。為了解決這個問題，我們提出了Lagrange，一種基於遮罩潛在場（MLF）的開放詞彙、計算稀疏的駕駛框架。Lagrange並不依賴於密集的體積重建或封閉集查詢機制，而是利用視覺-語言模型（VLMs）將類別無關的物體提議編碼為連續的語義視覺標記。我們引入了一個基於意圖的遮罩交叉注意模塊，該模塊在時間上過濾不相關的實體，將關注的標記解碼為定義在空間坐標上的隱式連續能量場。通過將決策制定框架化為跨越這一能量場的拉格朗日動作最小化問題，我們在執行碰撞避免的同時強制遵守車輛運動學的嚴格合規性。在標準（nuScenes）和長尾（CODA）基準上的廣泛離線評估表明，Lagrange建立了一個有前景的框架，用於穩健、可解釋且運動學上可行的開放世界自主駕駛。
2606.20274.author: Shihao Ji et.al.
2606.20274.authors: Shihao Ji, HongXi Li, Zihui Song, Mingyu Li
